# Daily Report — 2026-04-14

## Task Completed
Implemented the `create_user_criteria` tool (`user_criteria` table).

User Criteria records control who can see or request Service Catalog items,
categories, and catalogs. Each record specifies one or more conditions based
on user attributes — role, group, department, company, or location — and an
optional GlideScript for advanced logic. The `match_all` flag switches between
OR logic (any condition, default) and AND logic (all conditions must match).

Changes:
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/user_criteria_tools.py` — new module with
  `CreateUserCriteriaParams`, `UserCriteriaResponse`, and `create_user_criteria`
- `src/servicenow_mcp/tools/__init__.py` — exported `create_user_criteria`
- `src/servicenow_mcp/utils/tool_utils.py` — imported and registered the new tool
- `config/tool_packages.yaml` — enabled `create_user_criteria` in
  `catalog_builder` and `full` packages (removed TODO stubs)
- `tests/test_user_criteria_tools.py` — 14 tests covering minimal creation,
  each optional field, match_all flag, advanced script, inactive state,
  API endpoint correctness, HTTP/connection/timeout errors, and Pydantic models

## Test Results
- Total tests: 433 passed, 4 deselected
- Coverage: 88% overall; user_criteria_tools.py 100%
- All passing: yes

## Security
No known vulnerabilities found (pip-audit clean).

## Files Changed
- src/servicenow_mcp/tools/user_criteria_tools.py (new)
- src/servicenow_mcp/tools/__init__.py
- src/servicenow_mcp/utils/tool_utils.py

- tests/test_user_criteria_tools.py (new)
